page: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance type includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI), Intel Total Memory Encryption (TME), and hardware-accelerated cryptography features like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with Geekbench multi-core and image processing scores placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Passmark CPU encryption tests also rank in the top 25%. However, memory bandwidth benchmarks for large blocks show average performance, with read speeds at 163.82 GB/sec and write speeds at 70.41 GB/sec. While the instance achieves a strong rating for text generation with small 135M LLM models, text generation for large 70B LLM models falls into the bottom 25%, indicating a performance tradeoff for large-scale AI inference workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.

The m6id.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and high-capacity local NVMe storage, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space alongside substantial memory. It is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate web serving, but less optimal for large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, image processing, and encryption capabilities, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. However, large-block memory write bandwidth is moderate at 70.41 GB/sec, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is a notable tradeoff, ranking in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose applications requiring high-capacity local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, and strong multi-core processing capabilities.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, image processing, and encryption benchmark results
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
